title: Define Meshtastic peer metric exposure policy
description: Follow-up from project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.4 reviews: peer snapshots now carry last_heard_seconds_ago, rssi_dbm, and snr_db through the adapter API and gateway app_identity mapping, but NodeInfo emission only exposes name/device ID/hops. Decide whether these metrics belong in Meshtastic NodeInfo, another Meshtastic message, LICHEN status/CoAP, or remain internal; then add tests for the chosen wire/status behavior.
notes:
No-hardware policy decision (2026-07-02): keep peer last_heard_seconds_ago, rssi_dbm, and snr_db internal/native-only for now. Do not encode them into Meshtastic NodeInfo, do not synthesize MeshPacket.rx_rssi/rx_snr from peer-table state, and do not introduce Telemetry/NeighborInfo/NodeStatus exposure in this Bead. Explicit LICHEN peer hop_distance continues to map to NodeInfo.hops_away. Docs updated in docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md.

Tests added/updated: lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter/src/main.c adds a peer snapshot with last_heard/RSSI/SNR/hop_distance and asserts WantConfig NodeInfo emits identity/channel/DeviceMetrics/uptime/hops_away only; NodeInfo position/SNR/last_heard are absent using fixed32 checks for fields 4 and 5; DeviceMetrics battery/voltage/channel_utilization/air_util_tx are absent and uptime remains 0. lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter/src/main.c compares app_identity peer NodeInfo with and without metrics byte-for-byte and asserts peer metrics remain absent while hop_distance maps to hops_away.

Reviews: explorer Galileo recommended internal-only/native status policy; test-design Boole recommended negative wire-contract tests in meshtastic_adapter and meshtastic_gateway_adapter. Codereview Kuhn initially found P2 wrong wire type for gateway NodeInfo.snr; fixed and re-review reported no P0-P2 findings. Codereview Dalton initially found P2 missing DeviceMetrics fields 3/4 coverage and gateway NodeInfo.last_heard fixed32 mismatch; fixed and re-review reported no P0-P2 findings. Third codereview pending before closure.

Local checks: git diff --check passed after fixes.

EC2 validation: launched i-02c6d3a0d804f1600 in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr. Primary EBS worktree was dirty/behind, so validation used clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-0e37-validate from origin/main plus the local patch. Initial Twister run twister-out-0e37 failed in CMake because fresh host lacked gcc; installed host build prerequisites. Rerun command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out-0e37-rerun --inline-logs --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen. Result: 2 scenarios / 4 instances selected; 3 passed; 1 static skip; 0 failed; 0 errored; 0 warnings; 163 test cases executed; 31 skipped; duration 88.73s. Sizes: native_sim meshtastic_gateway_adapter text=245268 data=7135 bss=74924 dec=327327; native_sim meshtastic_adapter text=112377 data=1473 bss=40310 dec=154160; qemu_x86 meshtastic_adapter text=146832 data=4708 bss=115272 dec=266812.

EC2 cleanup complete: sync run, /mnt/lichen-zephyr unmounted, vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 detached and available, instance i-02c6d3a0d804f1600 terminated, temporary security group sg-0dde4929933846ea5 deleted.
Third codereview Hilbert reported no P0-P2 findings; reviewed docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md, lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter/src/main.c, and lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter/src/main.c; did not edit files or run tests.
